name: Tech Unity Cookout
description:
How pleasant it is when the people of tech come together in unity!

After a year of talking about it, we’re finally doing it: a chill summer cookout for everyone in Baltimore tech. No booths, no buzzwords — just food, sun, and a mic for any group that wants to say hi.

We are hoping to cultivate a virtuous environment of Baltimore's best tech leaders, to share organizational tips, and to lean into Smalltimore's unique social strengths in building the future economy. Ultimately, we hope to foster the relationships that create better lives for ourselves and our people.

Participating groups

* **Code Collective**
* **Baltimore Node Makerspace**
* **Baltimore Tech**
* **Brady UX**
* **DevOps Columbia**
* **Blk Tech Connect**
* **Baltimore Indie Game Devs**

Free admission. Food will be provided!

The event will occur at Wonderground Park in collaboration with the Greenmount West Community Association. It's nearby Guilford Hall Brewery, home of EquiTech Tuesday and occasional host of Baltimore Rhythm Festival.

In the case of inclement weather, the event will occur at Mount Vernon Marketplace, or at a local bar.

name: The #BaltimoreTech Happy Hour and Game night!
description:
This month, we will be meeting at RegionAle for happy hour and game night. This casual, recurring happy hour is for both new and seasoned Baltimore technologists interested in connecting with fellow tech enthusiasts and a diverse group of awesome people. Come grab a drink, plays some games and catch up!

Stay tuned on all Baltimore Tech Meetups on our new [Instagram - Baltimore Tech Meetup](https://www.instagram.com/baltimoretechmeetup/) or on Slack - [Baltimore Tech Meetup](http://baltimoretechslack.com/).

Date: May 22nd 2025
Location: RegionAle - 2400 Boston St. Suite 120, Baltimore, MD 21224
Time: 6:00pm to 8:00 pm

\#\#\# A few logistical details:

* We start at 6 PM. It usually lasts for a few hours - come and go when works for you.
* There is free parking in the parking lot in front.
* By attending, all participants commit to upholding and being subject to our [Code of Conduct](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ctYtmK0_q16rm7Vk3hdBkC6o_4MQZ_148bEKLR9JoCE/edit?usp=sharing).
* By attending, participants also acknowledge and grant permission for the use of photographs they may appear in for promotional material by the organizers or sponsors. If you would like to rescind this permission, please contact us directly.

name: Baltimore Code & Coffee Demo Day
description:
Our Demo Day is a chance for members of the local tech community to show off the cool projects they’ve been working on. Come and enjoy our participant’s ten minute demos with five minutes for questions.

We'll have demos on 3D Structural Optimization, GTM partnership automation and more.

After the demos, each of the participants will have their own table for you to ask more questions about their work in depth. After 30 mins of this, we will have a vote for the winner.

**SCHEDULE:**
Networking - 11:00am-11:30am
Demos - 11:30am-12:30pm
Table Talk - 12:30pm-1:00pm
Vote 1:00pm-1:10pm
Networking - 1:10pm - 2:00pm

**Venue:**
Digital Harbor Foundation
1045 Light St. Baltimore, MD 21230

**PARKING:** There is metered street parking. If all else fails, park at the West Street Garage. Residents of Baltimore near Charles St should consider the free Charm City Circulator bus.

**HOW TO FIND US**
Please use the main door at 1045 Light Street. If the door is closed, please ring the doorbell.

name: [IN-PERSON] Summer Game Dev Picnic
description:
Come join us for the Baltimore Indie Game Devs potluck picnic at BIG member Allen Lambert's house in Marriottsville just west of Baltimore. This picnic is for all local game devs in the Baltimore-Washington area. Allen will be manning the grill, and there will be outdoor games as well as tabletop games to play.

If you'd like to bring something to the potluck, please add it in the Comments section.

Notes:

* This is a NO ALCOHOL event.
* In the event of rain, we may postpone this event.
* **We'll send the street address to RSVP'd attendees one week before the picnic and the day before the picnic. Please send a message to Allen Lambert or Tony Li if you'd like the address sooner.**

name: Bitcoin Pizza Day Party with PizzaDAO
description:
Join us for a delicious celebration of Bitcoin Pizza Day, brought to you by PizzaDAO! 🍕💸

On this historic day, we honor the legendary first real-world Bitcoin transaction: when Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for two pizzas in 2010. That single cheesy exchange ignited a worldwide movement and earned a permanent slice in crypto history!

Join us for a delicious celebration of Bitcoin Pizza Day, brought to you by PizzaDAO! 🍕💸
On this historic day, we honor the legendary first real-world Bitcoin transaction: when Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for two pizzas in 2010. That single cheesy exchange ignited a worldwide movement and earned a permanent slice in crypto history!
🔥 **What to Expect:**
✅ **Casual networking** – Meet fellow crypto enthusiasts, builders, and investors.
✅ DeFi Demo – Presentation by local web3 developer who has built up $5 million in TVL
✅ **Good vibes, great beer** – No pitches, no panels, just **fun and great conversation** over drinks.
🚗 **Heads up: Parking is street parking only, so plan accordingly!**

name: Baltimore Neighborhood Economics Lab
description: The 2025 Baltimore Neighborhood Economics Lab is a powerful, day-long experience focused on Building a Just Economy in Uncertain Times. This convening brings together entrepreneurs, neighborhood leaders, organizers, funders, and policymakers from across the city and region to explore bold, community-rooted strategies for sustaining and growing our local economies, especially in the face of today’s economic and political uncertainty. Through interactive labs, thought-provoking panels, storytelling, and solution design, we will delve into the real challenges and creative opportunities surrounding capital access, shared ownership, local production, youth leadership, and emotional well-being. Together, we’ll imagine what’s possible when we center equity, care, and collaboration in the work of building vibrant neighborhood economies. We invite you to be part of this space—not just to listen, but to contribute, co-create, and connect.
